Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log and assign labels to phases likely initial idle then cutting assembling welding inspection packaging

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 1 3 Temperature 20 21 C pressure 1 bar constant vibration 0 Hz flow 0 units s tool position 0 mm Energy about 0 point 5 kWh This is idle or pre operation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 4 6 Temperature rises to 50 58 C pressure increases slightly 1 to 1 point 5 bar vibration increases from 30 to 38 Hz material flow remains at 5 units s tool position moves from 5 mm to 10 mm energy increases from 2 kWh up to 3 kWh This looks like a cutting operation high vibration and temperature So label Cutting

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 7 8 Temperature drops back down to 40 C pressure rises slightly 2 bar vibration low at 10 Hz material flow reduces to 2 units s tool position stays at 15 mm energy remains around 2 kWh

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Could be assembly or welding But high pressure maybe for welding But temperature lower than cutting but still high relative

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Actually welding would have high temperature 80 C maybe Lets see event 9 shows temp 80 vibration 50 but no flow tool position stays at 15 mm Energy spikes up to 5 kWh

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then event 10 temp 30 low vibration 1 no flow again Event 11 12 temp 25 small vibrations 5 material flow 1 unit s and tool pos 20 mm increasing gradually Then events 13 15 return idle again

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible activities Idle Setup Cutting Metal Assembling Parts Welding Quality Inspection Packaging

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But we only have one cycle of operations maybe with multiple steps Phase A Idle Setup Events 1 3 Phase B Cutting Metal Events 4 6 Phase C Assembling Parts Events 7 8

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Phase D Welding Heat Treatment Actually Event 9 shows high temp 80 and high vib 50 but no material flow could be Quality Inspection or Ultrasonic Test because no material flow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can interpret Event 9 as Welding because it uses heat and energy but not moving parts and no material flow may indicate the machine is not feeding new material during weld just heating existing part

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively Phase D could be Quality Inspection because after welding you inspect for defects using sensors that detect vibrations due to ultrasonic testing but there is still high temperature

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Typical manufacturing process for metal parts Step 1 Setup Idle Step 2 Cutting metal Step 3 Assembling parts Step 4 Welding parts together Step 5 Quality inspection using ultrasonic testing Step 6 Packaging

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However we do not see packaging step clearly in data so final mapping is Idle Setup Events 1 3 and 13 15 Cutting Events 4 6 Assembling Events 7 8 Welding Event 9 Cooling or Post weld Event 10 Final Adjustment or Packaging Prep Events 11 12

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Provide rationale referencing sensor patterns such as increased temperature vibration indicating cutting welding stable moderate conditions indicating assembly lack of material flow indicating inspection test step decreasing temperatures indicating cooling phase small flows indicating final packaging step
